zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Brewse於大約1376年出生在英國林可爾史爾吉爾斯比時，她的父親Sir John de Brewse46歲，母親Joan Shardelow42歲。她於1395年與Thomas Hawley在英國林可爾史爾吉爾斯比結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1406年之後在英國，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Brewse于大约1376年出生在英国林可爾史爾吉爾斯比时，她的父亲Sir John de Brewse46岁，母亲Joan Shardelow42岁。她于1395年与Thomas Hawley在英国林可爾史爾吉爾斯比结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1406年之后在英国，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Brewse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Montgomeryshire; also Norfolk): nickname for an irritating person, from Middle English brese ‘gadfly’ (Old English brēosa). See also Breeze , compare Breece .

German: habitational name from any of numerous places called Breese or Breesen, in Mecklenburg, Wendland (near Hanover), Brandenburg, and Pomerania. In some cases the placename is derived from a Slavic word meaning ‘birch’ (brěza in Old Slavic).

variants.name: Brese
meanings: metonymic occupational name from Middle Low German brēse ‘brooch, needle, clasp’, denoting a maker of such items.

variants.name: Breece
meanings: English (Montgomeryshire and Denbighshire): variant of   Breese  .

variants.name: Breeze
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire; also Norfolk, Montgomeryshire, and Lancashire): variant of Breese . The name migrated from Shropshire into Montgomeryshire. Some dictionaries derive it from the Welsh patronymic ab Rhys, but this is phonetically improbable, as ab would develop to ap before the voiceless r of Rhys, hence Preece . Compare Breece .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Brewse was born about 1376, in Girsby, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Sir John de Brewse, was 46 and her mother, Joan Shardelow, was 42. She married Thomas Hawley in 1395, in Girsby, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died after 1406, in England, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Brewse est née environ 1376, à Girsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Brewse, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Joan Shardelow, avait 42 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Hawley en 1395, à Girsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée après 1406, à Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Brewse um 1376 in Girsby, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Brewse 46 und ihre Mutter Joan Shardelow 42. Sie hat 1395 in Girsby, Lincolnshire, England Thomas Hawley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1406 in England und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Brewse è nata il circa 1376 nel Girsby, Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Sir John de Brewse, aveva 46 anni e sua madre, Joan Shardelow, aveva 42 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Hawley nel 1395, nel Girsby, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì dopo il 1406, nel England, e fu sepolta a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brewseが約1376年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアギルスビーで生まれた時，父Sir John de Brewseは46歳，母Joan Shardelowは42歳であった。1395年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアギルスビーでThomas Hawleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1406年以後にイギリスで死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brewse(이)가 대략 1376년 영국 링코실르 지르스비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Brewse(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Shardelow(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 1395년 영국 링코실르 지르스비에서 Thomas Hawley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1406년 이후 영국에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

mn.Grimsby, Lincolnshire, England: Grimsby, Lindon, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Brewse nasceu aproximadamente 1376, em Girsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Brewse, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Joan Shardelow, tinha 42 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Hawley em 1395, em Girsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu após 1406, em Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Brewse родилась около 1376 г. в местности гирсби, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Brewse было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Shardelow, 42. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Hawley в 1395 г. в местности гирсби, линколншир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла после 1406 г. в местности Англия и была похоронена в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Brewse nació aproximadamente 1376, en Girsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Brewse, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Joan Shardelow, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Thomas Hawley en 1395, en Girsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió después de 1406, en Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
